title: SD-JWT session tokens for off-chain proof reuse

artifacts.description: Mints SD-JWT tokens after verifyHandshake() succeeds, binding nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, expiry, and optional selective-disclosure claims with configurable TTL (60-3600s)
Verifies SD-JWT tokens, enforces requiredClaims presence, checks expiry, validates issuer signature, and returns decoded claims
SessionTokenConfig, SessionTokenClaims, SessionTokenResult, SessionVerifyOptions TypeScript interfaces
Barrel export for session token module
SD-JWT profile for Bolyra: claim namespace (bolyra.nullifierHash, bolyra.scopeCommitment, bolyra.permissionBits), TTL constraints, disclosure frame requirements, and binding to verifyHandshake output
Unit tests: mint with valid handshake result, TTL boundary enforcement, selective disclosure frame variations, invalid input rejection
Unit tests: verify valid token, reject expired token, reject missing required claims, reject tampered signature, requiredClaims partial match
Developer guide: quickstart snippet, TTL guidance, selective disclosure example, migration path from full ZK proof per request, security considerations (replay, revocation scope)

steps: Define spec/session-token-profile.md: pin SD-JWT draft version (draft-ietf-oauth-selective-disclosure-jwt-08), specify Bolyra claim namespace, disclosure frame schema, and binding rules to HandshakeResult fields
Add @sd-jwt/core (or jose + manual SD-JWT) dependency to sdk/package.json; audit for Apache-2.0 compatibility and add overrides entry if transitive conflicts arise

Implement sdk/src/session/types.ts: SessionTokenConfig (ttl, issuerKey, disclosureFrame), SessionTokenClaims (sub=nullifierHash, bolyra:scopeCommitment, bolyra:permissionBits, exp, iat, iss), SessionVerifyOptions (requiredClaims, clockSkew)

Implement SessionTokenIssuer.mint(handshakeResult, config): validate handshakeResult is verified, build SD-JWT payload from nullifierHash + scopeCommitment + permissionBits, apply disclosureFrame, sign with issuerKey (ES256 or EdDSA), enforce TTL clamp [60, 3600]
Implement SessionTokenVerifier.verify(token, opts): parse SD-JWT, verify issuer signature, check exp with clockSkew tolerance, assert all requiredClaims are disclosed, return typed SessionTokenClaims
Export SessionTokenIssuer and SessionTokenVerifier from sdk/src/session/index.ts and re-export from sdk/src/index.ts public API surface

Write unit tests for SessionTokenIssuer: happy path mint, TTL below minimum clamped or rejected, TTL above maximum rejected, missing handshake fields throw, disclosureFrame omits optional claims correctly
Write unit tests for SessionTokenVerifier: valid token passes, expired token rejects, tampered JWT body rejects, requiredClaims not disclosed rejects, clockSkew tolerance accepted
Run npm run typecheck and npm test in sdk/ to confirm no regressions

Write sdk/docs/session-tokens.md with a 5-line quickstart, explanation of which claims are always disclosed vs selectively disclosed, TTL guidance, and a note that session tokens do not replace on-chain nullifier checks for high-value operations
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md to reference session tokens as the recommended pattern for real-time API integrations
dependencies: HandshakeResult type from sdk/src/types.ts (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, permissionBits)
verifyHandshake() must have completed successfully before mint() is called
ES256 or EdDSA issuer keypair (caller-supplied; SDK does not generate or store keys)
